data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-95ec5ed5">
    <title>Implement axon Backend for bead tracker</title>
    <description>
Implement the axon-backed Backend (per ddx-6bb51a52 TD and ddx-bbdd7564 interface). Cover claim semantics, atomic writes, events append, archive policy, and JSONL export/import for bd/br round-trip. Selectable via the backend config knob. Behind a feature flag until ddx-3ec0chaos signs off.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go implements the Backend interface. 2. All ops (create/show/update/close, dep add/remove/tree, list/ready/blocked, claim, events append, archive split, import/export) covered by unit tests. 3. Selectable via beads.backend=axon. 4. go test ./cli/... passes with backend=axon for the new unit tests.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:beads, area:storage, kind:feature, backend-migration</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-5d49b14e" base-rev="fceccd453a4c64383de15c400e10429ad7d9f772" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260503T215823-b617b8de"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260503T215823-b617b8de/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260503T215823-b617b8de/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260503T215823-b617b8de/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260503T215823-b617b8de/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260503T215823-b617b8de/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Committed `5f1fbef7`. AC verification:

- **AC1** — `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` implements RawBackend (the storage half of the Backend interface; Store composes it via the same path JSONLBackend/ExternalBackend take). Compile-time check at `axon_backend.go:67` (`var _ RawBackend = (*AxonBackend)(nil)`).
- **AC2** — Unit tests cover Init, Create/Get, Update persistence, Claim/Unclaim, dep add/remove/tree, list/ready/blocked, AppendEvent splits into the events collection (TD-030 D3), Close + archive policy, JSONL export/import round-trip, atomic snapshot, concurrent claim serialization, and orphan-event recovery (`axon_backend_test.go`).
- **AC3** — Selectable via `beads.backend=axon` (`store.go:117-138`); gated by `DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL=1` until the chaos-test sibling signs off, with config-knob + env-var selection covered by `TestAxonBackend_SelectableViaConfigKnobWithFlag` and `TestAxonBackend_ConfigKnobIgnoredWithoutFlag`.
- **AC4** — `cd cli && CGO_ENABLED=1 go test ./internal/bead/... && lefthook run pre-commit` are both green.
